2606.11931.title: Semantic Grading of Written Answers in Low-Resource Language Bangla Using a Fine-Tuned Lightweight Language Model
2606.11931.paper_summary: Bangla is among the world's most widely spoken languages, yet it remains underserved in educational NLP research. In many remote and rural regions, access to qualified subject teachers is limited, and written answers are consequently graded largely by hand, restricting timely and consistent feedback. Automatic assessment is challenging because semantically correct responses can vary substantially in surface form. We present a bilingual (Bangla-English) evaluation system designed for low-resource educational settings that prioritizes semantic correctness over lexical overlap. Our approach fine-tunes a lightweight language model to grade each response using the question, reference answer, and student answer, producing a numeric score and concise, context-grounded feedback suitable for classroom deployment. We also construct a synthetic bilingual dataset to enable controlled training and evaluation. Across proprietary and open-source LLMs evaluated under a unified protocol, our QLoRA-tuned Qwen3-8B confirms consistent improvement by producing the most leakage-resistant feedback (RoRa = 0.819) in synthetic evaluation and the strongest agreement with human scores (rho = 0.936, MAE = 0.725) in a dedicated human study.
2606.11931.paper_summary_zh: 孟加拉語是世界上使用最廣泛的語言之一，但在教育自然語言處理研究中仍然受到忽視。在許多偏遠和農村地區，合格的科目教師的可獲得性有限，因此書面答案主要是手動評分，限制了及時和一致的反饋。自動評估具有挑戰性，因為語義正確的回答在表面形式上可能有很大差異。我們提出了一個雙語（孟加拉語-英語）評估系統，旨在低資源教育環境中，優先考慮語義正確性而非詞彙重疊。我們的方法微調了一個輕量級語言模型，利用問題、參考答案和學生答案來評分每個回答，生成數字分數和簡潔的、基於上下文的反饋，適合課堂使用。我們還構建了一個合成雙語數據集，以便進行受控的訓練和評估。在統一協議下評估的專有和開源大型語言模型中，我們的QLoRA調整的Qwen3-8B證實了一致的改進，通過在合成評估中產生最具抗漏性的反饋（RoRa = 0.819）和在專門的人類研究中與人類分數的最強一致性（rho = 0.936, MAE = 0.725）。
2606.11931.author: Meherun Farzana et.al.
2606.11931.authors: Meherun Farzana, Aniket Joarder, Mahmudul Hasan, Md. Mosaddek Khan
